_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 federal U.S. House 2026 candidates (priority: finish federal House). Baker MD-05 (still running, primary 6/23/26); Wood/Dunlap ME-02 (primary 6/9/26 went to RCV runoff, unresolved as of 6/15); Loud ME-02 LOST primary (last, ~10.1%) -> status lost; Prieditis MI-07 (still running, MI primary 8/4/26, petition stage, no citable positions). Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cites a URL; null where no evidence; no inference from party.

rushern-baker.notes_set: Conventional Maryland Democrat; former Prince George's County Executive (2010-2018). Still running for open MD-05 (Hoyer seat); MD primary 6/23/2026, not yet held (re-verify after). Endorsed MD same-sex-marriage referendum (Question 6) Oct 2012 and backed sexual-orientation anti-discrimination bills as a delegate -> contrary on marriage cells. As County Executive blocked county cooperation with federal immigration enforcement (sanctuary-type) -> contrary on border. One bipartisan-restraint point: 'only Congress can take this country to war' (campaign statement 2/28/26). No documented abortion position found and no abortion-industry endorsement either way; life cells null. No cross-party conservative deviation found.

0.note: Endorsed Maryland Question 6 same-sex marriage referendum (Oct 2012); does not affirm exclusively man-woman marriage.

1.note: Endorsed same-sex marriage referendum; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: As MD delegate supported sexual-orientation anti-discrimination bills; supports LGBTQ protections in policy.

1.note: As County Executive blocked county cooperation with federal immigration enforcement raids; 2026 attacked rival over ICE support. Opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Established sanctuary-type non-cooperation with ICE as County Executive.

0.note: Campaign statement 2/28/26: 'only Congress can take this country to war' -> affirms Article I war-powers requirement.

jordan-wood.notes_set: Consistently progressive first-time candidate (former End Citizens United VP; anti-corruption focus). ME-02 open seat; 6/9/2026 Democratic primary went to ranked-choice runoff (first-choice: Baldacci 32.7%, Wood 29.6%, Dunlap 27.5%, Loud 10.1%) -> RCV unresolved as of 6/15, Wood still in contention; re-verify nominee. Platform: Restore Abortion Rights Nationwide, Champion LGBTQ+ Equality, opposes indiscriminate mass deportations; endorsed by Brady PAC, Moms Demand Action, Maine Gun Safety Coalition (gun-control). No cross-party conservative deviation found; no documented conservative-aligned position.

0.note: Campaign issue: 'Restore Abortion Rights Nationwide' -> supports abortion rights, does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates restoring/expanding abortion rights -> contrary to abortion abolition.

0.note: Campaign issue: 'Champion LGBTQ+ Equality' -> does not affirm exclusively man-woman marriage.

1.note: Champions LGBTQ+ equality; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: Champions LGBTQ+ identity in public policy -> contrary to opposing its promotion.

1.note: Opposes 'indiscriminate' mass deportations -> contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.
Endorsed by Brady PAC, Moms Demand Action, and Maine Gun Safety Coalition (gun-control single-issue groups) -> contrary to opposing red-flag/AWB/magazine limits.

matthew-dunlap.notes_set: Current Maine State Auditor; former longtime Maine Secretary of State (chief election officer ~16 yrs) and state legislator -> real public record. ME-02 open seat; 6/9/2026 primary to RCV runoff, unresolved as of 6/15 (Dunlap ~27.5% first-choice, trailing, has NOT conceded); re-verify nominee. TWO cross-party deviations: opposes banning assault weapons (Maine Public Your Vote 4/19/26 'No') and supports War Powers Resolution / opposes 'dumb forever wars' on Iran. Election record cuts AGAINST a conservative election-integrity frame: opposes photo voter ID and is a pro-ballot-access, anti-voter-fraud-myth figure (sued the 2017 Trump fraud commission and won record release). Pro-abortion-rights ('100% PP rating', 20+ pro-choice votes) and pro-LGBTQ-equality.

0.note: Supports abortion rights ('Yes', 4/19/26); claims 100% Planned Parenthood rating.

1.note: States he 'voted more than 20 times to defend abortion rights' -> contrary to abolition.

0.note: Supports LGBTQ+ equality per platform; does not affirm exclusively man-woman marriage.

1.note: Supports LGBTQ+ equality; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: Platform supports LGBTQ+ equality -> contrary to opposing its promotion in policy.

3.note: As Maine SecState ~16 yrs expanded mail/absentee ballot access; sued the 2017 Trump voter-fraud commission and concluded fraud claims were a myth -> supports broad mail-in voting, contrary to opposing mass mail-in.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: opposes banning assault weapons ('No', 4/19/26) -> aligns with opposing AWB/magazine bans.

0.note: Calls for a War Powers Resolution before action on Iran and opposes 'dumb forever wars' -> affirms Article I congressional war-powers requirement.

paige-loud.notes_set: LOST the 6/9/2026 ME-02 Democratic primary -> finished last of 4 with ~10.1% (~5,743 first-round votes), eliminated in the ranked-choice runoff (Baldacci 32.7% / Wood 29.6% / Dunlap 27.5% led). No longer a viable nominee; status set to lost. Documented positions while a candidate: pro-abortion-rights, supports full LGBTQ equality, opposes photo voter ID. Genuine cross-party restraint deviation on foreign policy: would vote against any AUMF on Iran, opposes Taiwan intervention and 'indefinite, unchecked' Ukraine aid, 'reject the idea that strength comes from endless war.' (A Maine Public 'No' on banning assault weapons was flagged by research as needing exact-question verification -> left null.)

1.note: Supports abortion rights -> contrary to abortion abolition.

0.note: Supports 'full equality and protections' for LGBTQ individuals/couples/families -> does not affirm exclusively man-woman marriage.

1.note: Supports full LGBTQ equality; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

4.note: Supports LGBTQ equality in policy -> contrary to opposing its promotion.

0.note: Would vote against any authorization for military force against Iran -> affirms congressional war-powers check before action.

1.note: 'Reject the idea that strength comes from endless war, occupation'; opposes Taiwan intervention and 'indefinite, unchecked' Ukraine aid -> restraint, contrary to forever wars.

alexandra-prieditis.notes_set: Researched 2026-06-16: very thin footprint. Active but low-profile Democratic candidate for MI-07 (challenging Rep. Tom Barrett); MI primary 8/4/2026, still at petition-signature/pre-ballot-qualification stage. Field of 5 Democrats; EMILY's List endorsed rival Bridget Brink (not Prieditis) and no single-issue group endorsed Prieditis. Beyond broad slogans (personal freedom, healthcare-as-a-right, support local farmers, clean water/anti-PFAS, fair-share taxes) she has no documented, citable positions on any of the rubric issues and no candidate-questionnaire answers. All cells null -> nothing fabricated. No cross-party conservative deviation found.
